title: RubricRule
description: YAML-frontmatter contract for `rubric/<CATEGORY>/<NAME>-NN.md` files. Per-rule contract per .claude/rules/methodology.md § Per-rule contract. The detector engine loads rule files via this schema; the methodology codegen step (#7) emits webapp/src/generated/methodology/index.mdx from validated frontmatter. x-postgresql-skip: true because this is a file-format spec, not a persisted entity (the rule lives in git).

title.description: Plain-English headline for the finding (NO rule_id) — the `.fc-title` on every report. A human sentence fragment naming what was found, e.g. 'Fenced code block that tells the agent to run a shell command'. Flows through generate-methodology.cjs into webapp/src/generated/rules/content.ts.

explanation.description: The 'Why it matters' paragraph (`.fc-why`): 1–2 plain, second-person sentences on the risk + attack/outcome class. May use the closed placeholder set {match} {path} {line} {count} (interpolated at render with backend evidence; omitted gracefully when empty) and inline ….

severityRationale.description: Optional one-clause tie of severity to outcome — the `.fc-rationale` clause rendered after the severity word ('HIGH — …'). Omit for info-tier (advisory, no rationale).

categoryLabel.description: Optional human category label shown in the `.fc-rule` meta line (e.g. 'Prompt injection', 'Supply chain'). Falls back to the sub_score title when omitted.

action.description: Imperative one-line action that names the user's actual construct (`.fc-action`). For info-tier rules: 'No action required — context only.'.

steps.description:
Optional ordered remediation steps (rendered as `
    `). May contain inline ….

after.description: The 'Safer pattern' snippet (`.sp.after`). A pattern, NEVER a product/vendor (anti-recommendation per design-system.md).

saferPattern.description: Optional Avoid → Safer pattern before/after pair (`.fc-safer`).

remediation.description: Actionable remediation surfaced on every finding: `action` (required) + optional `steps[]` + optional `saferPattern`. Methodology-over-opinion — concrete and reproducible, never a product recommendation.

weight.description: Maximum penalty this rule contributes. 0 for advisory (`info`) and shadow rules.

status.description: Rule lifecycle state. New rules land `shadow` for 7 days; FP-audit gates promotion.

shadowUntil.description: ISO 8601 date when the FP-audit harness re-evaluates promotion. Required iff status=shadow; null otherwise.

appliesTo.description: Artifact types the rule fires against.

trigger.description: Closed set of 6 primitive trigger types. Adding a new primitive requires an RFC.

limitations.description: What the rule cannot catch. MANDATORY — no rule ships without limitations.

frameworks.description: Optional short framework-reference codes mapping the rule to an external AI-risk taxonomy (`owasp-llm:<id>`, `mitre-atlas:<id>`, `cwe:<id>`, e.g. `owasp-llm:llm01`, `mitre-atlas:AML.T0051`, `cwe:78`). Each code is resolved by the central FRAMEWORK_CATALOG in scripts/generate-methodology.cjs into a clickable {family,id,label,url} badge on the methodology card + scan-report findings; an unknown code hard-fails the generator. Omit where no honest AI-framework mapping exists (most maintenance/transparency/community rules). x-postgresql-skip already holds — no DB/Pydantic/Zod impact.

priorArt.description: URLs to CVEs / OWASP entries / research papers / vendor write-ups motivating the rule.

value.description: Number, string, or array depending on operator.

examples.title: Invisible tag-channel characters hidden in the text
examples.explanation: Unicode tag characters (U+E0000–U+E007F) render as nothing to a human reading {path}, but the agent still parses them — so a second, hidden instruction can ride along inside what looks like ordinary text and steer the agent without your knowledge.
examples.severityRationale: a hidden instruction can hijack the agent with zero visible trace in the file.

remediation.action: Strip the tag-channel characters and retype the affected line in plain ASCII.
steps: Re-type the highlighted line by hand — do not copy-paste it.
Add a Unicode-tag / zero-width linter to CI so this cannot silently reappear.

saferPattern.before: deploy the build  (with hidden U+E0001 tag chars between words)

limitations: Cannot detect tag-channel characters re-encoded as numeric entities (`󠀁`); rule operates on decoded text only.
Cannot detect tag-channel chars hidden inside compressed payloads — those are scanned only post-decompression.
